# Rekall — Claude Code + Obsidian Integration
# This file was generated by Rekall setup. Customize it to your needs.

## Obsidian Vault — Auto-Sync (MANDATORY, ALL CONVERSATIONS)

{{USER_NAME}} has an Obsidian vault. Save things there automatically during every conversation.

- **Vault path**: `{{VAULT_PATH}}`
- **Auto-save rule**: During every conversation, save noteworthy things to the vault AS THEY COME UP — don't wait until the end. Ideas, decisions, project directions, learnings.
- **About You (`About {{USER_NAME}}.md`)**: Update whenever you learn something new about {{USER_NAME}} — personality traits, opinions, preferences, reactions, things he cares about, how he thinks. Subjective observations count. If he says something revealing about himself, add it.

### Folder Placement (STRICT — no exceptions)

| Folder | What goes here | What does NOT go here |
|--------|---------------|----------------------|
| `Projects/` | Project hub notes ONLY — one per project, links to its research/decisions | Research, API docs, analyses |
| `Research/` | API references, market research, design research, library docs, how-to guides, comparisons | Project hubs, decisions, rough ideas |
| `Decisions/` | Choices made ("we chose X over Y because Z") + the analysis that directly led to the choice | Pure reference material, project hubs |
| `Ideas/` | Sparks, rough thoughts, concepts not yet a project | Research (if you're writing >1 paragraph of findings, it's Research/) |
| `Sessions/` | Daily session logs from Claude Code conversations — auto-generated by `/session-log` | Manual notes, research, decisions |

**Decision test**: "Does this note record a choice, or the analysis that directly informed a choice?" → Decisions/. Otherwise → Research/.
**Ideas vs Research test**: Is it a spark/concept (<1 page)? → Ideas/. Is it findings, data, or reference material? → Research/.

### Naming (STRICT)

- **Always Title Case with spaces**: `FastF1 Python Library.md`, NOT `fastf1-python-library.md`
- **No kebab-case**, no snake_case, no camelCase for filenames
- **Wikilinks must match filenames exactly**: `[[FastF1 Python Library]]`, NOT `[[fastf1-python-library]]`

### Frontmatter & Tags

- **Format**: YAML frontmatter with ALL of these fields:
  ```yaml
  ---
  date: YYYY-MM-DD
  tags: [type-tag, domain-tag]
  status: active
  summary: "One-line description of what this note contains"
  ---
  ```
- **Tag format**: Always use YAML arrays `[research, design]` — never `#hashtag` syntax in frontmatter, never bare comma-separated
- **Max 3 tags per note**: 1 type tag + 1-2 domain tags

**Required fields:**
- `date` — creation date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- `tags` — YAML array, 1 type tag + 1-2 domain tags
- `status` — one of: `active` (currently relevant), `stale` (related project inactive or info outdated), `archived` (historical, do not link to from new notes)
- `summary` — one-line plain-text description of the note's content (for fast scanning)

**Status rules:**
- When a project becomes inactive, cascade `status: stale` to all its related research/decision notes
- When scanning for context, skip `archived` notes — they are historical records only
- Do not create new links TO `archived` notes

**Allowed type tags** (pick exactly ONE):
`project`, `research`, `reference`, `decision`, `idea`, `session`

**Allowed domain tags** (pick 1-2 max):
`design`, `business`, `finance`, `dev`, `personal`, `knowledge-management`, `data-viz`, `email`, `job-search`

**Do NOT invent new tags.** If none of the domain tags fit, just use the type tag alone. If {{USER_NAME}} introduces a genuinely new domain, ask before adding a tag.

### Note Templates

**Research Note:**
```
# {Topic}
> [!summary]
> 2-3 sentence executive summary of findings.

Parent: [[Project Hub]]

## Key Findings
...

## Related
- [[Note]] — context

## Sources
- [Title](url)
```

**Decision Log (default):**
```
# {Decision Title}
Parent: [[Project Hub]]

## Context
What prompted this decision.

## Decision
What we chose and why.

## Consequences
What this means going forward.
```

**Decision Log (detailed — for complex multi-option decisions):**
```
# {Decision Title}
Parent: [[Project Hub]]

## Context
What prompted this decision.

## Options Considered
1. **Option A** — trade-offs
2. **Option B** — trade-offs

## Decision
What we chose and why.

## Consequences
What this means going forward.
```

**Idea Note:**
```
# {Idea Title}
One-paragraph description of the concept.

## Why This Could Work
...

## Open Questions
...

## Related
- [[Note]] — context
```

### Linking & Structure

- **Don't duplicate**: Before creating a new note, grep the vault for existing notes on the same topic. Update existing notes instead of creating duplicates.
- **Atomic notes**: One idea/topic per note. A note on "Gmail label system" is better than a giant "Email setup" note.
- **Linking rules**: Only link when the relationship is direct and meaningful. Sub-notes link to their immediate parent, not grandparent. Project hubs own their decisions/research.
- **Home.md**: Update ONLY when adding a new project or idea. Do NOT add decisions or research notes to Home — those belong linked from their project hub.
- **No orphans**: Every new note must have at least one `[[wikilink]]` to another note.
- **Parent links mandatory**: Every Research/ and Decisions/ note MUST have a `Parent: [[Project Hub]]` line. After creating one, update the project hub's Research or Decisions section to link back.
- **No root-level files**: Every .md file goes in a folder (except Home.md, About {{USER_NAME}}.md, and AGENDA.md).

### Automatic Session Logging

Session logging happens automatically via a SessionStart hook:
1. `session-logger.py` runs on startup, finds unprocessed Claude Code sessions, creates skeleton notes in `Sessions/`
2. If MEMORY.md shows "Pending Session Extractions", dispatch a **background agent** to fill in the skeletons:
   - Read each session note with `deep-extract: pending`
   - Find the matching session JSONL file in `~/.claude/projects/`
   - Extract: decisions made, things learned, ideas discussed, personal observations
   - Fill in the skeleton sections (replace `_pending deep extraction_` lines)
   - Update relevant project hubs with links to the session note
   - Update About {{USER_NAME}}.md with any personal observations
   - Update AGENDA.md with current context
   - Change frontmatter `deep-extract: pending` → `deep-extract: done`
3. `/session-log` remains available as a manual command for logging the CURRENT session before closing.

## Memory System — Instincts

The memory system uses `instincts.jsonl` as the source of truth. **MEMORY.md is auto-generated on session start — do NOT edit it directly.**

### Adding new instincts
When learning something new about the user's preferences or project patterns, append a new line to `{{MEMORY_DIR}}/instincts.jsonl`:

```json
{"id":"ins_NNN","pattern":"The learned pattern","domain":"domain-tag","confidence":0.5,"evidence_count":1,"last_seen":"YYYY-MM-DD","created":"YYYY-MM-DD","source":"observed","section":"Section Name"}
```

When Claude **observes** a pattern in user behavior (not explicitly stated), log it at confidence 0.3-0.5:

```json
{"id":"ins_NNN","pattern":"Observed pattern","domain":"domain-tag","confidence":0.5,"evidence_count":1,"last_seen":"YYYY-MM-DD","created":"YYYY-MM-DD","source":"observed","section":"Section Name"}
```

Only `user-explicit` patterns can reach 0.9. Observed patterns cap at 0.7 (after 3+ confirmations).

### Confidence levels
- `0.9` — User explicitly stated
- `0.7` — Observed 3+ times without correction
- `0.5` — Observed 1-2 times
- `0.3` — Tentative, single interaction
- Reduce by 0.1 if user corrects the behavior
- Auto-decays by 0.05 per 30 days if not re-observed
- Below 0.2 = removed from compiled MEMORY.md

### Updating instincts
- When a pattern is re-confirmed, update `last_seen` and increment `evidence_count`
- When adding a user-explicit preference, set `source: "user-explicit"` and `confidence: 0.9`
- Use next available `ins_NNN` id (check the last id in the file)

### Domain tags
`design`, `business`, `finance`, `dev`, `personal`, `knowledge-management`, `general`

### Section names
`Working Standards`, `User Preferences`, `General` (or create new sections as needed)
